name: GitHub
twoFactorHint: GitHub may prompt for a TOTP code, passkey, security key, or GitHub Mobile push. Check your authenticator app.
notes: Single page login. Arkose FunCAPTCHA triggers frequently on new IPs.

name: Google
captchaTextPatterns: this browser or app may not be secure
twoFactorHint: Google may prompt for phone tap, TOTP code, backup code, or security key. Check Google Authenticator or your phone for a push notification.
notes: Multi-step: email at /signin/identifier, then password at /challenge/pwd, then 2FA at /challenge/{method}. May show account chooser if multiple accounts. Aggressively detects automation - always use headed mode.

name: Microsoft
twoFactorHint: Microsoft may prompt for Authenticator push, TOTP code, SMS code, or phone call. Some accounts use passwordless (number matching) - you will see a 2-digit number to tap in the Authenticator app.
notes: Multi-step: email, then password or passwordless, then MFA. Shows 'Stay signed in?' (KMSI) page after auth - must click Yes or No to complete redirect. Enterprise accounts redirect to tenant URLs, not office.com - use ESTSAUTH cookie for reliable detection.

name: X (Twitter)
twoFactorHint: X may prompt for a TOTP code, SMS code, or security key. X may also ask you to confirm your username or phone number as an identity check before password.
notes: Multi-step SPA flow: identifier -> optional username challenge -> password -> optional 2FA. URL does not change between steps. Arkose FunCAPTCHA triggers on nearly every new IP. Most aggressive anti-bot of any provider.

name: Reddit
twoFactorHint: Reddit may prompt for a 2FA code.
notes: New Reddit (www.reddit.com/login) is a React SPA. Uses both reCAPTCHA and hCaptcha via A/B testing. TOTP-only 2FA.

name: Discord
twoFactorHint: Discord may prompt for TOTP, SMS code, or backup code. New accounts from suspicious IPs may require mandatory phone verification that cannot be bypassed.
notes: React SPA. Auth token stored in localStorage, NOT cookies. Playwright storageState captures both. Mandatory phone verification may trigger for new accounts - this requires human intervention.

name: Slack
twoFactorHint: Slack defaults to magic link (email code) instead of password. If you see 'Check your email', tell the user to enter the code from their email. TOTP 2FA may follow password login.
notes: Workspace URL required before login (<workspace>.slack.com/signin). Default auth is magic link (email code), not password. Password login may not be available. Enterprise Grid uses SSO/SAML redirect.

name: LinkedIn
captchaTextPatterns: let's do a quick security check
twoFactorHint: LinkedIn frequently triggers email/SMS verification for logins from new devices. This is not optional 2FA - it happens on virtually every new browser session. Tell the user to check their email or phone.
notes: Most traditional login form (server-rendered). Triggers email verification loop on nearly every new browser session. Use persistent browser profile to reduce verification triggers. JSESSIONID cookie value (with quotes) must be sent as csrf-token header for API calls.

name: GitLab
captchaTextPatterns: verify you are not a robot
twoFactorHint: GitLab may prompt for a TOTP code or security key. GitLab does not support SMS-based 2FA.
notes: Single page login with optional 2FA redirect. reCAPTCHA appears on sign-up more than login. Self-hosted instances may use LDAP.

name: Atlassian
twoFactorHint: Atlassian may prompt for a TOTP code, email verification, or security key.
notes: Multi-step: email first at id.atlassian.com/login, then password. Shared across Jira, Confluence, Bitbucket, Trello. After auth may redirect to product URL instead of start.atlassian.com - use cloud.session.token cookie for reliable detection.

name: AWS Console
twoFactorHint: AWS nearly always requires MFA. May prompt for TOTP code from authenticator app or hardware token tap. Enter the code quickly as TOTP codes rotate every 30 seconds.
notes: Multi-step: account type (Root/IAM) -> Account ID -> credentials -> MFA. IAM Identity Center (SSO) uses separate URL (<subdomain>.awsapps.com/start). Sessions expire in 1-12 hours depending on IAM settings.

name: Notion
twoFactorHint: Notion uses magic link (email code) as default login. No traditional 2FA. Google/Apple OAuth also available.
notes: Magic link (email code) is default auth. Password login available if user set one. After login, may show workspace selection page if user belongs to multiple workspaces.

name: Instagram
twoFactorHint: Instagram may prompt for a code sent via SMS, WhatsApp, or authenticator app. May also trigger 'suspicious login' email verification.
notes: Owned by Meta. Shares infrastructure with Facebook. Aggressive bot detection. May show 'suspicious login attempt' page requiring email/SMS confirmation. Rate limits login attempts aggressively.

name: Facebook
twoFactorHint: Facebook may prompt for a code from authenticator app, SMS, or a push notification to the Facebook app. May also show trusted device prompt.
notes: Single page login. Facebook checkpoint system may require photo verification, friend identification, or email confirmation for suspicious logins. Very aggressive anti-bot detection.

name: ChatGPT
twoFactorHint: OpenAI uses email-based login or Google/Microsoft/Apple OAuth. Email login sends a verification code.
notes: Uses Auth0-based flow at auth.openai.com. Cloudflare Turnstile on login page. Supports Google, Microsoft, Apple OAuth. Email-based login sends a code. May redirect through multiple domains (auth.openai.com -> auth0.openai.com -> chatgpt.com).

name: Dev.to
twoFactorHint: Dev.to primarily uses social login (GitHub, Twitter/X, Apple, Forem). No traditional 2FA.
notes: Primarily uses OAuth login via GitHub, Twitter/X, Apple, Forem. Email/password login also available. Built on Forem platform.

name: Hacker News
twoFactorHint: Hacker News uses simple username/password auth. TOTP 2FA available but rare.
notes: Minimal login page with no JavaScript framework. Simple HTML form. One of the easiest providers to authenticate with. Rate limited to prevent abuse.

name: Stack Overflow
twoFactorHint: Stack Overflow primarily uses Google, GitHub, or Facebook OAuth. Email/password also available.
notes: Part of Stack Exchange network. Shared auth across all Stack Exchange sites. Primarily OAuth-based (Google, GitHub, Facebook).

name: Vercel
twoFactorHint: Vercel supports TOTP 2FA and security keys.
notes: Login via GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, or email. Email login sends a verification code. Cloudflare Turnstile protection.

name: Netlify
twoFactorHint: Netlify uses GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, or email login. No traditional 2FA on Netlify's own auth.
notes: Primarily OAuth login via Git providers. Email/password also available.

name: Jira
twoFactorHint: Jira uses Atlassian ID. May prompt for TOTP or email verification.
notes: Uses Atlassian ID (same as atlassian provider). Use --provider atlassian for cloud instances. This entry is for users who search for 'jira' specifically.

name: Bitbucket
twoFactorHint: Bitbucket uses Atlassian ID. May prompt for TOTP or email verification.
notes: Uses Atlassian ID. Auth is identical to atlassian provider but redirects to bitbucket.org after login.

name: npm
twoFactorHint: npm may prompt for a one-time password from your authenticator app or a recovery code.
notes: Login via GitHub OAuth or email/password. 2FA is mandatory for publishing packages with >1M weekly downloads.

name: Docker Hub
twoFactorHint: Docker Hub may prompt for a TOTP code from your authenticator app.
notes: Uses Auth0-based login at login.docker.com. GitHub OAuth also available.
